summary: Question 4.28 asks when $X_R$ is prime, where $X_R$ is the Cayley graph on a finite ring $R$ built from its group of units. The source paper itself reduces the question to the semisimple case (non-semisimple $R$ yields non-prime $X_R$) and further to a combinatorial question about primality of tensor products of complete graphs. A March 2024 follow-up (arXiv:2403.05635) establishes necessary and sufficient conditions for primality in the related $p$-unitary Cayley graph setting, partially addressing the broader question for certain classes of finite commutative rings, but the full general question for arbitrary finite rings appears to remain open.

since_posted.title: On certain properties of the p-unitary Cayley graph over a finite ring
since_posted.authors: Authors not confirmed from abstract fetch
since_posted.claim: Establishes necessary and sufficient conditions for p-unitary Cayley graphs over finite commutative rings to be prime (under mild conditions), addressing a related but more restricted variant of Question 4.28.

search_queries: prime Cayley graph finite ring tensor product complete graphs Chudnovsky Spirkl 2024
arXiv 2401.06062 "prime Cayley" cited "X_R" finite ring primality 2024 2025
notes: The source paper (2401.06062) was published in the Journal of Combinatorics (2025/2026). The paper reduces Question 4.28 to the semisimple case and then to tensor-product primality. arXiv:2403.05635 (March 2024) provides necessary and sufficient conditions for the p-unitary Cayley graph to be prime, which is a related but distinct graph construction from X_R. A further paper arXiv:2603.21239 ('On U-unitary Cayley graphs over finite rings', March 2026) appeared in search results but could not be verified within the 5-call cap. Author names for 2403.05635 were not retrieved from the abstract fetch.
